Tassell]] (brother)
[[Jason Tassell]] (brother) }} }} '''Kris Tassell''' (born 16 September 1973) is a former [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. {{rlp|fb|wg}}, or {{rlp|ce}}, he played for the [[Canberra Raiders]], [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], [[North Queensland Cowboys]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and the [[Swinton Lions]]. ==Background== Born in [[Mount Isa]], Tassell, who is of [[Welsh people|Welsh]] descent through his grandparents, played his junior rugby league for the [[Cairns Kangaroos]] and attended [[Trinity Anglican School]] before being signed by the [[Canberra Raiders]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2019/04/05/league-community-mourns-passing-of-tom-tassell/|title=League community mourns passing of Tom Tassell|date=5 April 2019|website=QRL}} ==Playing career== In 1990, Tassell started at {{rlp|fb}} for the Queensland under-17 side in their 14–all draw with New South Wales.{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/representative/under-17s-origin/1990-2/|title=1990|website=18th Man|date=26 October 2017 }} In Round 10 of the [[1992 NSWRL season]], he made his first grade in the Raiders' 31–12 loss to the [[Parramatta Eels]].{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2018/06/21/forever-green-where-are-they-now---kris-tassell/|title=Where are they now? Kris Tassell|website=Canberra Raiders|date=21 June 2018 }} In 1995, Tassell joined the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], playing four first grade games, spending the majority of the season in reserve grade.{{cite web|url=http://thebulldogs.com.au/sportal.php?page=player&id=601|title=BULLDOGS RUGBY LEAGUE CLUB – OFFICIAL WEBSITE|website=thebulldogs.com.au}} In 1996, he returned to [[Queensland]], signing with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. That season, he scored eight tries in nine games before a knee injury against the [[Newcastle Knights]] in Round 11 ended his season. He finished the year as the club's equal-top try scorer.{{cite book |last=Cadigan|first=Neil|title=25 Years in the Saddle|year=2015|publisher=Playright Publishing|page=118|isbn=9780994237316}} In 1998, after just three games in 1997, Tassell became a regular starter for the Cowboys, playing 18 games on the wing. In 1999, his final year at the club, he played 12 games. In 2000, Tassell joined the [[Salford City Reds]] in the [[Super League]]. He played 26 games that season, mainly at centre, scoring seven tries. Later the year, he represented [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|Rugby League World Cup]]. In their first group game against the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]], he scored a hat trick and was named Man of the Match. In their semi-final loss to [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], he scored a try in the 22–46 defeat. In 2002, after two seasons with Salford, Tassell moved to [[Wakefield Trinity]], where he scored 10 tries in 24 games. In 2003, Tassell played for the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[2003 Rugby League National Leagues#National League Two|National League Two]]. colspan=2| Career totals ! 11 ! 6 ! 0 ! – ! 0 ! 24 |} ==Personal life== Tassell's older brothers, [[Brad Tassell|Brad]] and [[Jason Tassell|Jason]], were both professional rugby league players.{{Cite web|url=https://www.cairnspost.com.au/sport/first-joint-battle-for-tassell-brothers/news-story/591b6a510d0c3046504adec7119758d8|title=First joint battle for the Tassell brothers|date=24 November 2019|website=Cairns Post}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20070901235853/http://www.bulldogs.com.au/main.php?page=player&id=601 Bulldogs profile] *[https://web.archive.org/web/20081118201521/http://www.cymrurl.com//records.html Wales All time records] {{Wales squad 2000 Rugby League World Cup}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tassell, Kris}} [[Category:1973 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:20th-century Australian sportsmen]] [[Category:21st-century Australian sportsmen]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Canberra Raiders players]] [[Category:Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs players]] [[Category:North Queensland Cowboys players]] [[Category:Rugby league centres]] [[Category:Rugby league fullbacks]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Mount Isa]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]] [[Category:Salford RLFC players]] [[Category:Swinton Lions players]] [[Category:Wakefield Trinity players]] [[Category:Wales national rugby league team players]]
